instruction: Please set up a local Spotify app without sudo: install the provided spotify script to ~/.local/opt/spotify/spotify, create an executable launcher ~/.local/bin/spotify, and add desktop entry ~/.local/share/applications/spotify.desktop with Exec pointing to ~/.local/bin/spotify. After setup, running `spotify --version` must print `Spotify mock 2.0.0`.

parameters.command:
cat > /home/user/Desktop/installers/spotify_payload/spotify <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
if [ "$1" = "--version" ]; then
  echo "Spotify mock 2.0.0"
else
  echo "Spotify mock app running"
fi
EOF
chmod +x /home/user/Desktop/installers/spotify_payload/spotify

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5);

result.command: ok=1; [ -x /home/user/.local/opt/spotify/spotify ] || ok=0; [ -x /home/user/.local/bin/spotify ] || ok=0; [ -f /home/user/.local/share/applications/spotify.desktop ] || ok=0; [ "$(readlink -f /home/user/.local/bin/spotify 2>/dev/null)" = "/home/user/.local/opt/spotify/spotify" ] || ok=0; grep -qx 'Exec=/home/user/.local/bin/spotify' /home/user/.local/share/applications/spotify.desktop || ok=0; version=$(/home/user/.local/bin/spotify --version 2>/dev/null); [ "$version" = "Spotify mock 2.0.0" ] || ok=0; if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
